subject: Add swagger.json as discovery release artifacts
commit_message:
Add swagger.json as discovery release artifacts

Change-Id: I1c2d023dce35a4e74b7f2b0e91b8e3c0423b18da
Issue-ID: MSB-187
Signed-off-by: Lizi <li.zi30@zte.com.cn>

entries.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(2 comments)

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

why do you have to commit the swagger.json if it is generated or am I misunderstanding something. My impression was that the swagger schema is generated from the java code.

Patch Set 1:

swagger.json does not change once release, code is going to generate it every time the same json. to avoid this, swagger-sdk bring the cocept that generate once as part of build and use it ever. because ci does not have ability to check-in this json, we the community should put into the code base.
Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2:

(2 comments)

> swagger.json does not change once release, code is going to
 > generate it every time the same json. to avoid this, swagger-sdk
 > bring the cocept that generate once as part of build and use it
 > ever. because ci does not have ability to check-in this json, we
 > the community should put into the code base.

Thanks for your explanation :)

Patch Set 2:

recheck

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

I think there are two good options:
1. the REST API in java is generated from the json in this case the JSON is checked in
2. the JSON is generated from java POJOs, in this case the JSON is dynamically computed and attached to the JAR / attached as separate artefact. The JSON is not checked into the version control. To ensure that the JSON is in sync with the actual code.

Patch Set 2:

There is no need for the CI to "check-in" the JSON. It just needs to be released as separate artifact. You can use the maven attach artefact mechanism to "release" the JSON.

Patch Set 2:

> There is no need for the CI to "check-in" the JSON. It just needs
 > to be released as separate artifact. You can use the maven attach
 > artefact mechanism to "release" the JSON.

 > There is no need for the CI to "check-in" the JSON. It just needs
 > to be released as separate artifact. You can use the maven attach
 > artefact mechanism to "release" the JSON.

In this case, the swagger.json in main/resources do generated by java anotation. Locally, without the swagger.json in main/resources, need build two times to wrap it to the jar package. The first time, generate the swagger.json, and second, to pack it to jar. Certainly, I can try to find out the timming when the jar was built and generate the swagger.json before that, and then pack the swagger.json to jar.
But personally, I think the API should not change frequently after API frozen. If there do have some change the coder can merge the new swagger.json from local.

Patch Set 2:

no need for two phases simple use this to generate the json if I remember correctly it will be in the target/generated/... place https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-codegen/issues/6303

and use http://www.mojohaus.org/build-helper-maven-plugin/usage.html to attach an additional artefact or use http://techtraits.com/build%20management/maven/2012/06/24/Maven-Deploying-multiple-artifacts-from-one-build.html to add the second artefact or simply create a new folder in sdclient/schema and specify the dependency to your code and generate the JSON there.
